title: Sweet Friendly Korean Vanilla Cookies
description: These crumbly, vanilla-infused cookies blend Korean flavors with a low-sodium twist, perfect for kids and adults alike.

ingredients.name: vanilla sugar
ingredients.notes: already sweetened, use vanilla extract for extra aroma

ingredients.name: fermented rice powder
ingredients.notes: adds subtle umami and acts as a natural leavening aid

ingredients.name: egg yolk
ingredients.notes: binds the dough and adds richness

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a bowl, cream together butter, vanilla sugar, and honey until light and fluffy.
Add vanilla extract, egg yolk, and vanilla sugar again, beating until well combined.
Whisk flour, whole wheat flour, baking soda, salt, and fermented rice powder in a separate bowl.
Gradually fold dry ingredients into the wet mixture until a soft dough forms.
Roll dough into 1-inch balls and place 2 inches apart on the prepared sheet.
Flatten each ball slightly with the palm of your hand for a classic cookie shape.
Bake for 12-14 minutes or until edges are golden but centers remain soft.
Remove from oven and let cookies cool on the sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
